rootCause.summary: The 'Dropdown' link failed to navigate the user to the expected page, resulting in the user remaining on the original page.
triggerChain: Browser did not perform a successful navigation to the target URL
Page URL remained unchanged from the initial page
evidence: events[0].type: 'click', events[0].text: 'Dropdown', events[0].selector: '#content > ul > li:nth-of-type(11) > a'
events[0].pageUrl: 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/' (URL at the time of click)
url: 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/' (Final URL of the session, identical to start URL)
stats.navigations: 0 (Explicitly indicates no navigation events were recorded)
Absence of network requests for the main document to '/dropdown' or any other page after the click
rootCause.fix: Inspect the `<a>` tag for the 'Dropdown' link. Verify its `href` attribute is correctly set to `/dropdown`. If the `href` is correct, check for any JavaScript event handlers on the link or its parent elements that might be calling `event.preventDefault()` without subsequently initiating a new page load or client-side route change.

bugReport.title: 'Dropdown' link on homepage fails to navigate
bugReport.description: When the user clicks the 'Dropdown' link from the homepage (`https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/`), the page does not navigate to the expected 'Dropdown List' page (`https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/dropdown`). The session recording shows that no navigation occurred, and the user remained on the initial homepage URL. This indicates a broken navigation flow for this specific link.
timeline: 1776155187149: User clicked the 'Dropdown' link with selector '#content > ul > li:nth-of-type(11) > a'.
1776155201178: Session ended. The page URL remained 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/', indicating no successful navigation occurred.
bugReport.networkActivity: No network requests for the main document were observed after the click that would indicate a navigation attempt or failure. Several analytics XHRs to 'log.optimizely.com' were made, some of which contained 'n=' parameters suggesting page contexts like '/dropdown', '/status_codes', and '/status_codes/404', but these are not direct evidence of main document navigation.
bugReport.consoleLogs: No console errors or warnings were recorded during the session.

environment.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/147.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

steps: Open a web browser and navigate to 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/'.
Scroll down the list of examples and locate the 'Dropdown' link (it's the 11th item in the list).
reproductionSteps.expectedBehavior: After clicking the 'Dropdown' link, the browser should navigate to 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/dropdown', displaying the 'Dropdown List' page.
reproductionSteps.actualBehavior: After clicking the 'Dropdown' link, the browser remains on 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/', and the content of the homepage is still visible. No page navigation occurs.
